title: bonnaya verbenifolia
intro: Bonnaya verbenifolia, also known as Kavani or Wild Chirata, is a tropical plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, characterized by its green, lance-shaped leaves and small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid and glycoside content. The health benefits of Bonnaya verbenifolia include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are often used to make decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
